title: pyrus scalaris
side_effects: Pyrus scalaris can have side effects if used improperly, such as rapid heartbeat, dizziness, and nausea due to its high concentration of cardiac glycosides. Ingesting too much of the plant's extract can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, while prolonged use may cause kidney damage and liver toxicity. Some individuals may also experience allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling, especially if they are allergic to other plants in the same family. In severe cases, excessive consumption of Pyrus scalaris can even trigger seizures or comas, requiring immediate medical attention.
